about.summary: Attia Youseif is a Senior Lecturer of Arabic at Indiana University. His work focuses on Arabic linguistics, Arabic language pedagogy, Arabic dialectology, Arabic cultural instruction, and technology-based learning materials for Arabic learners. He has taught Arabic across multiple proficiency levels and developed content-based Arabic courses on media, cinema, minorities in the Middle East, contemporary Arab issues, business communication, and Arabic language and culture.

featured_highlights: Senior Lecturer of Arabic at Indiana University
Director of Tutoring of the Arabic Flagship Program
Author and co-author of books on forensic linguistics, Qur’anic vocal performance, Arabic grammar, and teaching Arabic with computers
Developer of Arabic content courses, including Minorities in the Middle East, Arab Media, and Contemporary Challenges in the Arab World
Researcher in Arabic dialectology, corpus-based Arabic learning tools, and computational analysis of Arabic literary texts
Recipient of Indiana University teaching awards and multiple teaching and research grants

teaching.teaching_summary: Youseif teaches Arabic language courses across proficiency levels and content courses that combine language development with culture, media, politics, literature, and contemporary issues in the Arab world. His teaching emphasizes communicative language instruction, cultural competence, student engagement, project-based learning, and advanced academic discourse in Arabic.
content_courses_taught: Contemporary Challenges in the Middle East
Daily Life in the Arab World
Introduction to Arabic Language and Culture I
Introduction to Arabic Language and Culture II
Crash Course in Arabic Business Communication and Marketing

new_courses_created.title: Culture and Identity: Taste of the Middle East
new_courses_created.description: A course designed to help new university students understand cultural identity, cultural diversity, stereotypes, and cooperative college life through examples from the Middle East.

new_courses_created.title: Global Classroom: Minorities in the Middle East
new_courses_created.description: A collaborative Arabic course connecting Indiana University, the American University in Cairo, and the American University in Beirut. The course studies ethnic and religious minorities in the Middle East through academic and non-academic sources.

new_courses_created.title: Crash Course in Arabic Business Communication and Marketing
new_courses_created.description: A short course on Arabic business language, cultural competency, practical vocabulary, hierarchy, nonverbal communication, and marketing to Arab consumers.

new_courses_created.title: Introduction to Arabic Language and Culture II
new_courses_created.description: A three-week short course introducing Arabic conversation, numbers, time, family vocabulary, and selected aspects of Arab culture.

new_courses_created.title: Introduction to Arabic Language and Culture I
new_courses_created.description: A three-week short course introducing greetings, common expressions, self-introductions, hobbies, and basic Arab culture.

new_courses_created.title: Daily Life in the Arab World
new_courses_created.description: An advanced fifth-year Arabic content course on education, work, social interaction, family life, cuisine, health, and social customs.

new_courses_created.title: Contemporary Challenges in the Arab World
new_courses_created.description: An intensive advanced Arabic language and culture course focused on contemporary Arab culture, debate, analysis, criticism, and academic discourse.

new_courses_created.title: Minorities in the Middle East
new_courses_created.description: An Arabic content course on ethnic and religious minorities in the Middle East and Arab world from historical, cultural, social, political, literary, and media perspectives.

new_courses_created.title: Arab Media
new_courses_created.description: An Arabic content course focused on reading, listening, discussion, presentation, and composition through Arab online media and current socio-political and cultural issues.

summer_language_teaching.role: Arabic Instructor and Assistant of Arabic Head Session

service.service_summary: Youseif has contributed to Indiana University, the Arabic Flagship Program, the Center for the Study of the Middle East, student organizations, ACTFL, IFLTA, Middlebury College, and the broader Arabic teaching profession through committee work, advising, event organization, proposal review, and professional leadership.

technology.technology_summary: Youseif’s technology-related work connects Arabic pedagogy, corpus-based learning resources, interactive digital materials, computational analysis of Arabic texts, and online language learning environments.

projects.name: Arabic Listening Station
projects.description: A website for Arabic listening materials from multiple sources, organized by proficiency level and weekly class topic, with automated graded assignments.

projects.name: Naguib
projects.description: A lexical conceptual taxonomy for the computational analysis of Arabic novels.

projects.name: SALAM
projects.description: A series textbook and website for non-native Arabic speakers, with a first book designed for Japanese students.

projects.name: ArabiLight
projects.description: A context-sensitive, conversation-friendly model for spoken Modern Standard Arabic.

projects.name: MASHHOOR
projects.description: A learner’s familiarity dictionary for Arabic using the web as corpus.

projects.name: Al-Kindi
projects.description: A model for author attribution in Arabic religious and historical speeches.

projects.name: Thamaraat
projects.description: Corpus-driven dictionaries and models for learning and teaching Arabic.
